_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning that a simple sentence is one idea, made of one group of words that goes together.

_source.w: We are learning that a simple sentence is one idea, made of one group of words that goes together.
y: Understanding sentence structure helps us write clear, complete ideas.
Simple sentences are the building blocks of all good writing.
When we know what makes a sentence work, we can check our own writing.
eg: We identify simple sentences in a text, like 'The cat sat on the mat'.
We practise writing one complete idea in each sentence.
We check our writing to make sure each sentence expresses a whole thought.
We sort a group of words into 'sentences' and 'not sentences'.
We build simple sentences using subject-verb cards.
We read a sentence aloud and decide if it sounds complete.
